perturbation.original_text: 13. Governing Law and Venue . The internal laws of th e State of Florida, without regard to principles of conflicts of laws, sha ll govern the validity and interpretation of this Agreement, and the performance by the Parties of their respective duties and obligations hereunder. The Parties irrevocably and uncond itionally consent to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Florida and of the Un ited States of America located in the State of Florida, County of Hillsborough, for any ac tions, suits or proceedings arising out of or related to this Agreement. The Parties shall not commence any action, suit or proceeding relating to this Agreement except in such courts. The Part ies agree that they may be served with process and receive notices at their respective addresses set forth on Exhibit B attached to this Agreement. The Parties irrevocably and unconditionally waive a ny objection to the laying of venue in any of the aforementioned courts and shall not plead or claim in any such court that an action, suit or proceeding brought in such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text:
22. Drafting . No provision of this Agreement shall be interpreted for or against either Party on the basis that such Party was the dr aftsman of such provision, both Parties having participated equally in the dr afting hereof, and no presumption or burden of proof shall arise favoring or disfavoring a ny Party by virtue of the authorship of any of the provisions of this Agreement.

13. Governing Law and Venue . The internal laws of th e State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of laws, sha ll govern the validity and interpretation of this Agreement, and the performance by the Parties of their respective duties and obligations hereunder. The Parties irrevocably and uncond itionally consent to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Florida and of the Un ited States of America located in the State of Florida, County of Hillsborough, for any ac tions, suits or proceedings arising out of or related to this Agreement. The Parties shall not commence any action, suit or proceeding relating to this Agreement except in such courts. The Part ies agree that they may be served with process and receive notices at their respective addresses set forth on Exhibit B attached to this Agreement. The Parties irrevocably and unconditionally waive a ny objection to the laying of venue in any of the aforementioned courts and shall not plead or claim in any such court that an action, suit or proceeding brought in such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to the 'Drafting' section, a section typically unrelated to legal jurisdiction, reduces its prominence and clarity. Also, by changing Florida to Delaware, there is a contradiction with the location where the Parties consent to.
perturbation.law_explanation: 28 U.S.C. § 1391 dictates that venue is proper in (1) a judicial district where any defendant resides, if all defendants reside in the same state; (2) a judicial district where a substantial part of the events or omissions giving rise to the claim occurred, or a substantial part of property that is the subject of the action is situated; or (3) if there is no district in which an action may otherwise be brought as provided in this section, any judicial district in which any defendant is subject to the court's personal jurisdiction with respect to such action. By placing the governing law and venue clause in an unexpected section and modifying the state of governing law, its enforceability becomes uncertain, and goes against this general provision of venue.

Venue generally (a) Applicability of Section .-Except as otherwise provided by law- (1) this section shall govern the venue of all civil actions brought in district courts of the United States; and (2) the proper venue for a civil action shall be determined without regard to whether the action is local or transitory in nature. perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original contract by changing the governing law from Florida to Delaware while still specifying Florida as the venue. This creates a conflict because the governing law typically influences the interpretation and enforcement of the contract, including venue. The Law Explanation discusses 28 U.S.C. § 1391, which deals with venue, and the Perturbation Explanation highlights that the clause was moved and modified, making its enforceability uncertain. Snippet 1, referencing 28 USC 1391, indicates that this section governs venue in civil actions, reinforcing that the altered clause could conflict with established venue guidelines.
